sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The board didn't change after clicking those purple dots (board_changed: false). This means clicking the same purple dot again doesn't do anything, or the purple dots can only be clicked once. Since I already clicked the top-center purple dot and it revealed the white squares, clicking the other purple dots didn't change anything.
Looking at the current state, the bottom-right grid still shows the same white squares as before. Let me now click all the white squares to turn them gray, and see if that completes the level.
The white squares are at:
- Top center: rows 49-51, cols 29-31 → click at row 50, col 30
- Middle left: rows 54-56, cols 24-26 → click at row 55, col 25
- Middle right: rows 54-56, cols 34-36 → click at row 55, col 35
- Bottom center: rows 59-61, cols 29-31 → click at row 60, col 30
Let me click all 4 white squares.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click all 4 white squares in the bottom-right grid
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 50, 'col': 30},  # top center
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 25},  # middle left
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 35},  # middle right
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 60, 'col': 30},  # bottom center
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
